Identity.History: Ardashir I ruled from 224–242 AD and founded the Sasanian Empire after overthrowing the Parthian Arsacid dynasty. Beginning as a local ruler in Persis, he defeated the last Parthian king, Artabanus IV, and claimed the ancient Iranian title Shahanshah, “King of Kings.” Ardashir centralized royal authority, promoted Zoroastrianism, reformed administration, and revived Achaemenid-style imperial ideology. He campaigned against Rome and expanded Persian control across Mesopotamia and Iran. His reign laid the political, military, and religious foundations of the Sasanian state.
